text:
3. **Transport** is one global append-only `~/.proactive-context/logs/events.jsonl` (broker and per-project logs rejected for zero-infra/discovery reasons); concurrency is safe via `O_APPEND` + sub-`PIPE_BUF` lines (payloads capped, briefing stored as length+summary), no write-path locking; throttled best-effort rotation under a rare flock.
4. **Correlation** uses process-global `project`/`session_id`/`req` slots so `run_query`/`rerank_hits` emit with zero new params; daemon rotates `req` per index pass. `log_event(event, lat_ms, payload)` is best-effort, all failures swallowed; no new crate deps (OnceLock/RwLock, nix flock, hand-rolled fixed-width RFC3339 enabling lexicographic `--since`). [...] **3. `proactive-context tail`** — a colorized, cross-project live view backed by a best-effort global event log (`~/.proactive-context/logs/events.jsonl`, atomic `O_APPEND` sub-`PIPE_BUF` writes, no hot-path locking). Per-request 3-char ID gutter + per-project color, `-q/-v/-vv`, `--grep`, `--project`, `--since`, `--json`, and lossless non-TTY degradation. [...] 4. **Latency is the headline metric** since inject is the hot path: total wall time on every `inject.done`, per-stage breakdown at `-v`, amber/red thresholds for slow injects. Capture is visually marked as the slow lane (`S`-prefixed IDs, diamond glyphs) so its 15s timings don't read as errors. [...] 1. **The event taxonomy is one request's lifecycle, not a flat list** — `inject.start → query.start → retrieve.* → generate.* → inject.done` maps 1:1 onto the `generate.rs` pipeline. The end-to-end trace is the spine; quiet/busy/capture/error views are all derivations of it.
